mission.title: Install and Configure CloudNativePG (PostgreSQL Operator)
mission.description: CloudNativePG is an operator for managing PostgreSQL databases within Kubernetes environments. It automates the entire lifecycle of PostgreSQL clusters, making it ideal for teams looking to integrate database management into their Kubernetes workflows. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and the reliance on Kubernetes-native features for high availability.

steps.title: Add the CloudNativePG Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official CloudNativePG Helm chart repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the operator.

```bash

helm repo add cloudnative-pg https://charts.cloudnative-pg.io

helm repo update

```

> This adds the repository for CloudNativePG.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the CloudNativePG operator
steps.description:
This step installs the CloudNativePG operator in the 'cloudnative-pg' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace cloudnative-pg

helm install cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --version 1.28.1

```

> Creates a namespace for the operator.
> Installs the operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a PostgreSQL cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a PostgreSQL cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  patroni:
    postgresql:
      version: "14"
EOF

```

> Creates a PostgreSQL cluster named 'my-postgres'.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  patroni:
    postgresql:
      version: "14"
EOF

steps.title: Verify the PostgreSQL cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the PostgreSQL cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

kubectl describe cluster my-postgres -n cloudnative-pg

```

> Lists the PostgreSQL clusters in the 'cloudnative-pg' namespace.
> Describes the details of the 'my-postgres' cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the CloudNativePG operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the CloudNativePG operator and its resources. Be aware that this will delete the PostgreSQL clusters and any associated data.

```bash

helm uninstall cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg

kubectl delete namespace cloudnative-pg

```

> Uninstalls the operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the CloudNativePG operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your PostgreSQL data. This step upgrades the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o yaml > backup-clusters.yaml

helm upgrade cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --version 1.28.1

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

```

> Backs up the current cluster configurations.
> Upgrades the operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the clusters are still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o yaml > backup-clusters.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources in the Kubernetes cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your Kubernetes nodes have enough resources (minimum 2 vCPU, 4GB RAM).

troubleshooting.title: PostgreSQL cluster is not ready
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may be in a pending state due to configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the operator using 'kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloudnative-pg -n cloudnative-pg'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the PostgreSQL service is not running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the service status with 'kubectl get svc -n cloudnative-pg' and check the cluster status.

troubleshooting.title: Failed to apply CRD
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the CRD is not installed or is outdated.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRD using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/main/deploy/crds/postgres.cnpg.io_clusters.yaml'.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for PostgreSQL 15, added new metrics for monitoring.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.27.x, ensure to review the new metrics configuration.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME          READY   STATUS    AGE' with your PostgreSQL cluster listed as 'my-postgres' and its status as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  patroni:
    postgresql:
      version: "14"
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
